mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC remaining below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment rate crossing the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer and investment demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week during economic expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during expansion phases, with out-of-sample directional accuracy dropping below 52% over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 2.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% threshold, invalidating the tightening fear channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, supporting corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or zero Rank IC for 60-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth indicates stronger aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a recessionary phase signals collapsing factory output and impending earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during recessions fail to predict negative 20-day returns, with positive returns occurring in > 55% of observed contraction months.
